statement.markdown: Solve the problem and fix the bugs.
statement.inputFormat: Input will contain multiple test cases.
statement.outputFormat: For each test case, output the dequeued element.
statement.notes: Note that there is only one element in the queue.

editorial.title: Dequeue Logic
editorial.intuition: A queue is a linear data structure that follows the FIFO (First-In-First-Out) principle. In a queue, you can insert or remove elements only from the front.
editorial.bruteForce: A brute-force solution would involve removing the front element and shifting the remaining elements to the front.
editorial.betterSolution: For a queue, you can dequeue by removing the first element.
editorial.optimalSolution: An optimal solution would use a deque data structure to efficiently dequeue elements.
